course_content_summary: Financial mathematics, actuarial sciences, statistics, computational methods, probability theory, stochastic, seminars, reading courses, project seminars, Insurance Economics, Economics of Banking, Dynamics of Financial Markets.

Plastics Technologies, Additive Manufacturing, Materials Science of Metals, 3D Metal Printing, Manufacturing Technology, Materials Science of Plastics, Powder Technology, Structure Optimisation, Design Rules for Additive Manufacturing, Product Creation, Standard Software Application Development.

program_name: Advanced and Computational Mathematics – International Master's Programme

accepted_tests.min_score: Listening and Reading Test 785 points, Speaking Test 160 points, Writing Test 150 points

english_requirements.notes: Completed degree in English Studies or American Studies; Completed degree with English as the language of instruction; Proof of professional qualification as a state-examined interpreter/translator; Applicants from countries with English as official/educational or native language are not required to submit proof of English language proficiency with their application.

german_requirements.notes: Although not compulsory, basic knowledge of German is recommended at the beginning of the programme. Courses to obtain the required level of German language (A2) are included within the curriculum for non-native speakers.

course_content_summary: The Advanced and Computational Mathematics Master's programme at Chemnitz University of Technology focuses on solving real-world problems through advanced mathematics. Students develop expertise in advanced mathematics, preparing them for careers in academia and industry. The curriculum includes a solid foundation in advanced mathematics, followed by specialization in one of three fields: Advanced Pure Mathematics, Computational Mathematics, or Data Sciences. The program also includes a seminar, a Master's thesis in the chosen specialization, and mandatory German language courses. High-achieving students may qualify for a PhD program in Mathematics.

program_name: Advanced Architecture — From Urban Design to Building Construction

english_requirements.notes: Native English speakers as well as graduates from English-language Bachelor's programmes are exempt.

course_content_summary: Advanced Architecture program focusing on design and construction across all scales, from urban contexts to single details. Topics include urban design, building design in existing contexts, built environments, and new buildings. Emphasizes internationally-oriented projects, elective modules, and Master's theses. Students develop skills to translate abstract designs into concrete implementations, combining professional, practical, technical, economic, ecological, and social aspects, considering cultural, historical, and climatic situations. Course objectives include developing future-oriented concepts through study projects, office internships, and collaboration with research institutes and construction partners. Graduates are prepared for roles in architectural, engineering, and construction companies, property development, housing associations, real estate, public sector, and R&D in design and construction, particularly focusing on execution planning and its connection to scientific research.

program_name: Advanced Computational Methods in Civil Engineering

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: at least 20 CP each must be demonstrated from two of the areas listed below

accepted_tests.test_name: RWTH Aachen Language Center Placement Test

english_requirements.notes: Placement test of the RWTH Aachen Language Center can only be obtained by individuals who are already enrolled as students at RWTH Aachen.

course_content_summary: Advanced Computational Methods in Civil Engineering (AdCom) focuses on applying and developing numerical methods and simulations for innovative civil engineering structures. The interdisciplinary curriculum includes courses from Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Mathematics, covering structural mechanics, fluid mechanics, and numerical mathematics. Students learn to master challenges with new resource-conserving materials, advanced composite materials, and lightweight structures subject to dynamic loads. Expertise includes numerical methods, mechanical principles, software code development, and assessing numerical modelling limits. The program is highly scientific and research-oriented, preparing graduates for PhD programs and scientific careers in research or industry, with a strong focus on research and international reputation.

english_requirements.notes: Completed degree in English Studies or American Studies; Completed degree with English as the language of instruction; Proof of professional qualification as a state-examined interpreter/translator; Applicants from countries with English as official/educational or native language are not required to submit proof of English language proficiency within their application.

german_requirements.notes: Courses to obtain the required level of German language (A2 by the end of the third semester) are included within the curriculum for non-native speakers. Students providing a certificate of A2 level (CEFR) may choose advanced language courses or subject studies.

course_content_summary: Advanced chemical and physical aspects of modern materials, synthesis, development, analysis, manufacturing of functional materials. Interdisciplinary program based on materials chemistry and materials physics. Research-oriented extension and intensification of physical and chemical knowledge, advanced courses in modern research fields, lab-oriented modules, research project, Master's thesis.

German language courses at level A2 will be offered at our university. German language attendance certificates will not be accepted. It is also possible to take an online course before the semester starts (440 EUR fee, details in the admission letter).

Proof of a Bachelor’s degree in English from Australia, Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, USA, United Kingdom, South Africa accepted. Other language certificates may be accepted following a case-by-case assessment.

course_content_summary: The Master's degree program in Advanced Materials at Justus Liebig University Giessen focuses on modern functional materials modified at the molecular level for specific applications. It addresses questions related to extending the service life of electric car batteries, increasing solar cell efficiency, and developing biocompatible implants. The program integrates interdisciplinary knowledge from chemistry and physics to understand and change material properties, emphasizing creativity and practical optimization in the laboratory. It provides theoretical and practical knowledge to tailor materials for future applications, with a strong integration into current materials research focusing on high-performance and energy materials. Students can work on current research projects and choose a specialization. The program offers excellent career opportunities and the unique opportunity to obtain a double degree with partner universities like Osaka University (Japan), Kansai University (Japan), and the University of Padua (Italy).

required_degree_domains: Engineering Science with a major in Materials Science
Natural Science with a major in Physics
Natural Science with a major in Chemistry
course_content_summary: Advanced Materials Analysis (AMA) Master's program for materials science, engineering science, physics, or chemistry graduates. Focus on behavior and analysis of solid materials down to atomic level, combining natural scientist's and engineer's perspectives. Utilizes various instrumental analysis techniques for materials characterization, structure-property relations, and targeted tailoring. Covers analysis of advanced steels, electronics materials, shape memory alloys, and energy materials using microscopic, spectroscopic, and diffraction methods. Prepares graduates for careers in materials-related industrial fields, research and development, metallurgy, and semiconductor industry, as well as academic research.

english_requirements.notes: Native speaker exempt, English-language Bachelor's degree accepted, English as a first language at German Abitur level accepted.

course_content_summary: Master's programme in Advanced Quantum Physics including experimental and theoretical courses. Fields covered: quantum optics, quantum matter, laser cooling, quantum gases, laser physics, superconductors, Josephson junctions, open and many-body quantum systems, mathematical quantum theory, quantum information theory. Practical content in quantum labs: lasers, elements of quantum optics, superconductors, photons, photon statistics. Students choose modules from a neighbouring field (minimum 6 ECTS) or complete an industrial internship.

required_degree_domains: Related subject with equivalent chemistry content

program_name: African Verbal and Visual Arts: Languages, Literatures, Media and Art

english_requirements.notes: Language skills can also be proven through a final dissertation or equivalent written papers in English.

german_requirements.notes: Basic German knowledge (level A1) is required and can be acquired during the first year of the programme.

course_content_summary: The English-taught Master's programme in African Verbal and Visual Arts deepens knowledge in diversity and creativity of languages, literatures, art, and media in Africa. It is interdisciplinary, combining subjects selected individually. Students can learn two African languages (Bambara, Swahili, Wolof, Arabic, Xhosa). For Swahili Studies, a B1 level of Swahili is compulsory. The program covers theories, methods, and thematic foci for cultural studies and humanities in Africa, building on specializations like African Linguistics, Swahili Studies, Literatures in African Languages, African and Afrophone Philosophy, and Media/Arts and Curational Studies in Africa.

german_requirements.notes: not a prerequisite, but recommended

course_content_summary: The Agricultural and Food Economics (AFECO) Master's program at the University of Bonn is an internationally focused postgraduate study. It provides knowledge and skills for awareness, analysis, and development of market activities along the agricultural/food value chain, from farm production, processing, trading, to food consumption. Graduates are prepared for executive positions in businesses, organizations, and political institutions within the global food system, as well as for high-level research. The program offers major specifications: 'Agribusiness' covers management issues, financial accounting, business analysis, production planning, investment appraisal, financial and risk management, quality management, and marketing. 'Agricultural and Development Policy' focuses on economic behavior in agri-food and rural economies, global food systems, and the impacts of agri-food and development policies. 'Market and Consumer Research' applies economics and management principles to analyze food markets and consumer behavior, including food policy, trade, risk management, market structure, consumer behavior theories, and market research tools. 'Resource and Environmental Economics' addresses renewable/non-renewable resource use, environmental valuation, food demand's environmental influences, and national/international environmental policies.

required_degree_domains: life sciences with a focus on plant and/or animal sciences
course_content_summary: The Master's program in Agricultural Biosciences at TUM focuses on securing the world's food supply, preserving natural resources, and mitigating climate change effects. It covers biological disciplines relevant for crop and livestock production, integrating molecular, biochemical, physiological, genetic, and genomic knowledge with quantitative and computational approaches. The program emphasizes basic biological research for efficient and environmentally-friendly agricultural production, understanding crop and livestock species from cellular to population levels. It adopts a generic approach, studying plants, animals, and microbes, and leverages synergistic effects from integrated studies. A strong focus is placed on molecular and computational methods, reflecting innovation in agricultural biosciences. Graduates gain a profound understanding of biological processes for agricultural production, research skills, awareness of societal challenges, and social skills for interdisciplinary communication and complex project management. Career paths include universities, research institutes, public sector, biotech, breeding, life science companies, and start-ups in biotechnology and machine intelligence.

Research-based and methods-oriented program in Agricultural Economics (AgEcon) focusing on economic, agricultural, and development policy relationships within the agricultural and food economy, environment, and rural areas. Central topics include economic growth, sustainability, and social justice in international and development-related contexts. The curriculum provides comprehensive education in economic and agricultural theories and methodologies, with application to specific problems in the international agricultural and food sector. Key competencies developed are theoretical concepts of agricultural economics, quantitative and qualitative research methods, integration of sustainability issues into policy analysis, interdisciplinary analysis of complex systems, and communication and presentation techniques.

program_name: Agricultural Sciences and Resource Management in the Tropics and Subtropics (ARTS)

english_requirements.notes: Proof that the BSc was taught in English is also accepted.

german_requirements.notes: No German language requirement

course_content_summary: Agricultural production, natural resources, complex interactions, human activities, interventions, biophysical, socioeconomic background knowledge, research-oriented, multidisciplinary approach, basic and applied management of natural resources, agricultural development, research, (sub)tropical environments, problem recognition, problem solving, (sub)tropical resource management, agricultural development, agricultural and environmental research, development positions, PhD programmes, cooperation with development organisations, universities, research institutions, holistic, cross-disciplinary understanding, structure, use, interactions, endangerment, protection of natural resources, internships, employment.

program_name: Agricultural Sciences in the Tropics and Subtropics (AgriTropics)
course_content_summary: Agricultural Sciences in Tropics and Subtropics (AgriTropics) is an international Master's program focusing on development-oriented agricultural research. It addresses challenges like population growth, climate change, and resource depletion, aiming for agroecological, sustainable, and highly productive agriculture. The curriculum provides broad knowledge in tropical agriculture, combining interdisciplinary approaches to train students in development-oriented agricultural research and prepare them to lead multidisciplinary teams. Key skills include specific knowledge in development-oriented agricultural research, competence in multidisciplinary and interdisciplinary cooperation, assessment of natural resource influence on agricultural production in tropical and subtropical agroecosystems, evaluation of material cycles (water, nitrogen, carbon), reduction of post-harvest losses, promotion of innovation in product processing, management of gender-sensitive development and extension approaches for sustainable agricultural production, improving marketability of agricultural products, increasing rural incomes, and applying integrated knowledge for management recommendations and sustainability strategies in the context of climate change. Graduates find opportunities in international/national agricultural research centers, ministries for development cooperation, land/environmental protection, consulting firms, NGOs, universities, research institutions, agricultural businesses, and EU institutions. Internships are recommended, and the program qualifies for doctoral studies.

program_name: Agricultural, Food and Environmental Policy Analysis (AFEPA)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: including one course each in microeconomics and statistics

course_content_summary: European Master's program in Agricultural, Food and Environmental Policy Analysis (AFEPA). Focus on socio-economic and environmental effects of agricultural, food, and environmental policies. High-quality education in policy design and assessment. Specializations: agri-food and policy, environmental and natural resource policy, agribusiness and market analysis, market and consumer research, development policy. Research-oriented curriculum, preparing for doctoral studies. Double degree program with partner universities.

english_requirements.notes: Previous studies conducted entirely in English accepted.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in "Agriculture, Ecology & Societies" (AGES) explores sustainable agriculture from an interdisciplinary perspective. Students combine courses in natural sciences, biophysical basis for sustainable agroecologies, ecological land and farm management practices, social organisation, governance, economics of agriculture, rural geographies, rural-urban linkages, political demands, food system, agricultural beliefs, ethics, critical food systems research, and advocacy. The program emphasizes interdisciplinary, critical, and project-based learning, focusing on sustainable, equitable food systems and training change-makers with interdisciplinary skills for food system transformation. Expertise from the Faculty of Organic Agricultural Sciences, Humanities, Social Sciences, Economics and Management, and Architecture, City and Landscape Planning is integrated. Specialization opportunities exist in agroecology, agricultural development, and environmental governance.

The Master's degree program in AgriFood Economics, Policy and Regulation focuses on interdisciplinary and cross-cultural skills to address conflicts in the international agricultural and food sector. Key topics include biodiversity preservation, climate change, economic and sustainable development, food security, fair supply chains, and international integration of agricultural markets and policymaking. The curriculum covers the complexity of the AgriFood sector, integrating biological, technological, economic, social, and political-legal challenges. Students will explore digital technologies, resource utilization, sustainable regional production and marketing, empirical research methods, business, and politics. Graduates will understand ecological implications, socio-economic and political interactions, technological principles of food production, and be able to analyze scientific reports, communicate with stakeholders, lead multidisciplinary teams, resolve conflicts, promote cooperation, and develop innovative solutions like consumer initiatives, business models, cooperative approaches, campaigns, and new regulatory systems. The program aims to train analysts and problem solvers who can find compromises between economic and ecological needs in modern food systems.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of university entrance qualification or completion of an English-language Bachelor’s degree course from Australia, Ireland, Canada, Aotearoa/New Zealand, the USA, the UK, or South Africa is accepted.

course_content_summary: Agrobioinformatics MSc program focuses on cutting-edge tools and techniques to solve global challenges like food insecurity and climate change. It involves programming and analyzing Big Data, combining bioinformatics methods with agricultural issues, bridging computer science, biotechnology, and modern agriculture. The curriculum provides comprehensive computational training to analyze and interpret data from genomics, proteomics, metabolomics, and other biological disciplines. Topics include basics of programming, quantitative genetics, and development of analysis pipelines. Students gain insights into plant protection, seed production, and agrobiotechnology services such as genotyping, phenotyping, sequencing, and double haploid production.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of university entrance qualification or completion of an English-language Bachelor’s degree course of study from Australia, Ireland, Canada, Aotearoa/New Zealand, the USA, the UK, or South Africa is accepted.

course_content_summary: Biotechnology, global economic development, international competitiveness, genomic research, cell biology, cell factories, technological advances, plant production, animal production, nutritional sciences, academic training, agricultural sectors, food sector, practical methods, scientific foundation, research-oriented study, problem-solving in farming practice, international expertise, creativity, innovation, assessment of technological processes.

course_content_summary: Highly interdisciplinary study program focusing on mitigating biases in AI, enhancing user-dependent explainability and interpretability, ensuring data privacy and security, equitable access to AI technologies, and fostering a competitive AI ecosystem. Covers legal, ethical, and policy aspects of artificial intelligence, preparing students to shape regulations and governance frameworks, assess potential risks, and contribute to responsible, ethical, and inclusive AI applications. Addresses current developments in AI, including generative AI for texts, speech, images, and videos, with an emphasis on interdisciplinary oversight supported by social scientists to understand AI workings, limitations, and impact.

program_name: AI-Driven Mechatronics and Robotics (Mechatronics and Robotics Study Track)

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Prerequisite Subject Areas from Bachelor's Degree

english_requirements.notes: More information on accepted certificates can be found on the university website.

course_content_summary: In-depth understanding of complex mechatronic systems, functions, mechanics, electrical engineering, computer science. Scientific analysis, interaction of mechanical, electrical, information technology components. Design, modelling, construction, implementation, testing of mechatronic systems. Development of innovative mechatronic systems, optimisation, disciplinary resources. Independent scientific work, research planning, critical analysis. Integration of mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, computer science methods to solve technical problems.

The AI-Driven Supply Chain Management (MSc) program at Hof University of Applied Sciences prepares students for dynamic global supply chains. It provides a comprehensive understanding of supply chain concepts, enabling analysis of complex logistics networks, evaluation of innovative methods in logistics and procurement management, and application of AI-driven technologies to optimize efficiency, sustainability, and resilience in end-to-end value creation networks. Aimed at graduates of business-related Bachelor’s programs (e.g., Business Management, International Management, Industrial Engineering, Business Informatics) with an interest in digital and AI-driven supply chain management, the program imparts theoretical expertise and hands-on skills to ensure supply security, flexibility, and long-term competitive advantages. The curriculum covers international value chain management, digital procurement, AI-driven logistics, supply chain analytics, automation, digital identification, business process management, data science, applied AI concepts, and global business strategy. Graduates will be able to design, analyze, optimize, and digitally transform global supply chains, enhance efficiency, sustainability, and resilience using AI-driven solutions, implement data-driven decision-making in procurement, logistics, and supply chain management, and manage cross-functional, AI-powered, and international supply chain networks. Career prospects include leadership positions such as AI-Driven Supply Chain Manager, Supply Chain Analyst, Logistics and Operations Manager, Procurement and Sourcing Manager, Digital Transformation Manager, Supply Chain and Digital Innovation Manager, Sustainability and Risk Manager in Supply Chains, Digital Consultant in SCM, and Digital Operations Manager.

program_name: Air Quality Control, Solid Waste and Waste Water Process Engineering (MSc WASTE)

german_requirements.notes: International students without prior knowledge of German must attend the intensive German language course in September (free of charge).

course_content_summary: The Master of Science in Air Quality Control, Solid Waste and Waste Water Process Engineering (MSc WASTE) is a four-semester, international English-taught program. It focuses on fundamentals of environmental and process engineering through compulsory advanced modules. Students specialize in Air Quality Control, Solid Waste, or Waste Water Process Engineering, each including a core module, elective modules, and practical work or an industrial internship. The program allows for individual study plans, broadening or specializing studies, excursions, and practical work in research facilities. The fourth semester is dedicated to the Master's thesis, which can be done at the university, a company, or another research facility. German language courses are provided for students without prior proficiency, with alternative interdisciplinary modules for those exempt. A double degree program with Universidade Federal do Paraná (UFPR) in Brazil is also possible.

Air transport, logistics, global transport systems, aircraft, aviation industry, technical knowledge, operational knowledge, logistic knowledge, economic knowledge, legal knowledge, air transport systems, analyse air transport processes, optimise air transport processes, problem-solving, graduate academic level, engineering, natural science, transport economics, operational aspects, planning aspects, transport system theory, aviation specialists.

The MA American Studies programme offers sophisticated expertise in US culture, literature, history, society, and politics. It focuses on advanced academic skills and research capabilities, preparing students for academic careers or international employment in fields such as cultural affairs, media and communication, publishing, political and international organisations, science and research management, or adult education. The program deepens understanding of the United States from an interdisciplinary perspective, allowing specialization through individual choices in thematic modules and research projects.

The two-year Master's program in American Studies offers comprehensive training in methods and theories of American Studies, covering literatures, cultures, history, and society of the United States. Courses range from America's colonial past to contemporary themes in race, class, gender, religious, ethnic, and regional studies. The curriculum emphasizes student autonomy, academic collaboration, and interdisciplinary exchange. Key areas include American Literature and Culture, Interdisciplinary Profile, Research, and Practice. An introductory module builds common knowledge and research/writing skills. The core curriculum focuses on fundamental theoretical frameworks. Students attend courses in other departments to create unique academic profiles and discuss MA thesis projects in a colloquium. The program emphasizes professional experience, offering opportunities for applied American Studies through organizing exhibitions, festivals, workshops, or tutoring. Internships outside academia are encouraged. Graduates find job opportunities in academia, journalism, publishing, public relations, consulting, international organizations, new media, and public administration.

Applied & Environmental Geoscience (AEG) is an international, research-oriented, interdisciplinary Master's program focusing on the evaluation and solution of environmental problems, particularly in the subsurface. Key areas include pollution of drinking water supplies, non-sustainable use of natural resources, and the impact of climate and land-use change on soil and water quality. The curriculum integrates classical natural sciences (mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology) with modern technology to understand physical, chemical, and biological mechanisms. Core fields covered are hydrogeology, environmental modelling, environmental chemistry, environmental microbiology, and environmental physics. Specializations are offered in Hydrogeology, Environmental Chemistry and Environmental Microbiology, and Environmental Physics. The program emphasizes multidisciplinary approaches and independent problem-solving.

program_name: Applied AI for Digital Production Management, MEng

english_requirements.notes: Specific requirements are available on the university's website.

german_requirements.notes: Recognised German language certificate recommended for non-Lisbon convention applicants. Compulsory German lessons are part of the program.

course_content_summary: The Applied Biotechnology Master's programme combines profound knowledge of methods and professional expertise from biotechnology with skills in data analysis, research management, and quality management. Topics include bioprocess engineering, protein purification, cell biology, food production, analytics, statistics, quality and research management, bioeconomy, and technology assessment. Elective courses from business, engineering, and natural sciences are available, including German language classes. The program concludes with a Master's thesis.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: At least 60 credits in Computer Science and Applied Computer Science, and at least 15 credits in Foundations of Mathematics and Natural Science.

modules.subject_area: Computer Science and Applied Computer Science
Foundations of Mathematics and Natural Science

english_requirements.notes: English-language skills must be demonstrated by the minimum passing grade in an internationally recognised test, or by equivalent proof. The test may not be taken later than two years prior to submission of the application.

course_content_summary: Students may choose one programme specialisation from one of two concentrations: Systems-Oriented Concentration (Applied Systems Development, Bioinformatics, Geoinformatics, Ecological Informatics, Medical Informatics, Law and Computer Science, Business Informatics, Scientific Computing, Computational Neuroscience, Digital Humanities) or Application-Oriented Concentration (Bioinformatics, Geoinformatics, Ecological Informatics, Medical Informatics, Law and Computer Science, Business Informatics, Scientific Computing, Computational Neuroscience, Digital Humanities, Data Science).

english_requirements.notes: We may also personally interview applicants in order to test their English skills. If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: Curriculum includes International Project Management, Software Architecture, IT Security, Advanced Database, Software Development Practice, Advanced Computer Science. Students are guided into standard areas of full stack development (front end, middleware, back end / databases and infrastructure) and individual specialisations (Business Computing, Mobile Computing and Usability Engineering and UX Design). Graduates work in software and IT industry for companies like ABB, Airbus, Accenture, Audi, Bosch, BMW, BASF, Deutsche Boerse, DKFZ, Triumph, PWC, SAP, Verivox, and SMEs. Qualifies for public sector, research, and doctoral studies.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics or related field
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Minimum 45 ECTS required for application; remaining 15 ECTS can be completed after admission.

english_requirements.notes: Alternatively, B2 CEFR is accepted in combination with German DSH level 2. Test results must not be older than two years.

german_requirements.notes: Required only if English proficiency is proven at B2 level.

course_content_summary: Data scientists extract knowledge from data, combining skills in mathematics, computer science, statistics, and application domains. The program emphasizes interdisciplinary data science, providing in-depth knowledge of key mathematical, statistical, and computer science methods. Application domains include Computational Neuroscience, Bioinformatics, Medical Data Science, Digital Humanities, Computational Sustainability, Digital Business Administration, and Physics. It is research-oriented, enabling autonomous scientific research projects, communication of insights, and reflection on ethical impacts of data collection, analysis, and automated data-driven decision-making. Opportunities for internships and industry interactions are also provided.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants who have studied completely in English in their previous studies may have the test waived. Applicants may also be personally interviewed to test English skills.

course_content_summary: Applied Data Science and Analytics, business application data scientist, technical courses, statistical courses, big data architectures, data mining algorithms, text mining algorithms, neural networks, predictive analytics, creative questioning techniques, visualisation, data storytelling, ethics, international law, practical experience, case studies, Master's thesis.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Attained in economics (e.g., business administration, economics, methods of economics, business informatics, business mathematics, business education)
Acquired through one or more term papers or seminar papers that meet scientific standards, in addition to a written thesis.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of sufficient English language skills is not required if the Bachelor's programme was taught entirely in the English language.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in Applied Economics prepares students for careers in the international economic arena. It focuses on developing key competencies for modern economists, including independent evaluation and analysis of data, and deriving policy suggestions from a solid theoretical foundation. The curriculum combines theoretical, institutional, and empirical analysis with a focus on real-world and global economic issues. Students learn to apply empirical, econometric, quantitative, and experimental methods to identify causal economic relationships, critically assess empirical studies, and draw conclusions. The program emphasizes the role of theory in empirical research and fosters an international perspective on economic action, preparing graduates for roles in business, consulting, or politics through human capital formation, networking, and international activities.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: at least 30 ECTS CP in Maths, Physics, Chemistry, and/or Biology
as well as a geological field-mapping course

course_content_summary: The Applied Geosciences Master's programme at the University of Bremen provides in-depth knowledge in applied geosciences, focusing on complex tasks related to terrestrial topics. Students can compile an individual study profile using elective modules in fields such as engineering geology, hydrology, applied sedimentology, geophysics, geohazards, renewable energies, and glaciology. Methodological knowledge is supplemented by competence modules. The program fosters a collaborative culture and offers opportunities to work with leading institutions like the Fraunhofer Institute for Wind Energy Systems, the Alfred Wegener Institute for Polar and Marine Research, and the Bremen State Geological Office.

program_name: Applied Geosciences – Applied Mineralogy and Crystallography (AMC)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Required for applicants with a Geosciences Bachelor's degree (e.g., geosciences, earth sciences, geology, mineralogy, crystallography). Bachelor's degree must have at least 180 ECTS.

modules.condition_comment: from biology, chemistry, mathematics and/or physics
e.g. geology, mineralogy, geochemistry, crystallography, geoengineering

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Required for applicants with a Materials Science/Engineering or Chemistry/Physics Bachelor's degree (e.g., materials science, chemistry, materials engineering, physics). Bachelor's degree must have at least 180 ECTS.

modules.condition_comment: e.g. mineralogy, petrology, analytical chemistry, crystallography, materials science, solid state physics, solid state chemistry

english_requirements.notes: Proof of English language skills required at enrolment (not application) according to RWTH Aachen University regulations. Refer to university website for accepted certificates.

course_content_summary: Applied Mineralogy and Crystallography (AMC) specialisation focuses on theoretical knowledge and practical training in structure, chemistry, and properties of geomaterials, functional materials, energy materials, and nanomaterials. Investigates mineralogical processes, develops materials with tailored properties for technological applications (e.g., battery components). Strong emphasis on experimental and analytical techniques, extensive laboratory training, microscopy methods. Elective field trips, research opportunities at synchrotron radiation facilities and neutron sources. Modules include diffraction methods, laboratory courses, electron microscopy, geochemistry, data management, chemistry, and materials engineering. Prepares graduates for academic research and industry careers in geosciences and materials science.

program_name: Applied Geosciences – Computational Geology and Geophysics (CGG)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: One of two alternative profiles. Bachelor of Science or Engineering with at least 180 ECTS required. Specific ECTS from the following areas:

modules.subject_area: Natural sciences (biology, chemistry, mathematics and/or physics)

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Profile in Physics or Data Sciences
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: One of two alternative profiles. Bachelor of Science or Engineering with at least 180 ECTS required. Specific ECTS from the following areas:

modules.subject_area: Physics and/or data science (theoretical physics, solid state physics, quantum mechanics, programming, data structures & algorithms, databases & information systems, computer engineering, computability & complexity, formal systems, automata & processes)

english_requirements.notes: Proof required at enrolment, not application. Refer to RWTH Aachen University regulations for accepted certificates.

course_content_summary: The Applied Geosciences MSc program, specifically the Computational Geology & Geophysics (CGG) specialization, offers interdisciplinary training in computational geosciences. It focuses on understanding the Earth through advanced computational techniques and geophysical methods. Students develop skills to analyze complex geological data, model subsurface structures, and interpret geophysical data, with a strong emphasis on theoretical foundations and practical applications. The program addresses real-world challenges in sustainable resource exploration, renewable energy, and geodata science, integrating cutting-edge technology with traditional geosciences. The curriculum includes compulsory and elective modules covering basic geoscientific knowledge, geothermics, applied geophysics, programming, computer-aided analysis and visualization of spatial data, statistics, numerical modeling, and artificial intelligence. The program concludes with a Master's thesis.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: at least 20 ECTS total from Biology, Chemistry, Mathematics, and/or Physics
at least 80 ECTS total from subjects like Geology, Mineralogy, Geochemistry, Crystallography, Geoengineering

english_requirements.notes: Proof required at enrolment, not application. See external link for accepted certificates.

german_requirements.notes: Not required as language of instruction is English.

course_content_summary: Applied Geosciences, Energy & Mineral Resources (EMR), exploration, production, energy resources, mineral resources, sequestration, storage, Geothermics, Shallow Geothermal Systems, Sustainable Organic Resources, Subsurface Analysis, Characterisation, Advanced Structural Geology, Economic Geology, Mineral Exploration, Resource Estimation, Applied Geochemistry, Petrology, Applied Seismology, Underground Excavation, Disposal of Radioactive Waste, Repository Design, Geophysical Imaging, Monitoring Methods, Geophysical Logging, Log Interpretation, Plate Tectonics, Structural Geological Models, Applied Biogeochemistry, Geostatistical Modelling, Remote Sensing, Sedimentology.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of English language skills according to RWTH Aachen University regulations at enrolment. See external link for accepted certificates: https://www.fgeo.rwth-aachen.de/cms/geowissenschaften-und-geographie/Studium/Studierende/Angewandte-Geowissenschaften-und-Georess/~lemb/Sprachkenntnisse/lidx/1/

course_content_summary: The Applied Geosciences (FBG) MSc program offers an immersive, practice-oriented approach to Earth study, combining traditional geological methods with modern tools. Key features include an extended field camp for hands-on experience in geological mapping, structural analysis, and field documentation, emphasizing fieldwork skills like planning, safety, teamwork, and professional conduct. The program provides a strong theoretical foundation in geosciences and develops analytical skills through lab-based courses and data interpretation. Students learn modern techniques such as drone-based surveying, satellite remote sensing, and digital mapping. Graduates are prepared for careers in research, resource exploration, environmental consulting, and natural hazard assessment.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: from biology, chemistry, mathematics and/or physics
e.g. geology, mineralogy, geochemistry, crystallography, geoengineering

english_requirements.notes: Proof of English language skills required at enrolment (not application). Refer to university regulations for accepted certificates: https://www.fgeo.rwth-aachen.de/cms/geowissenschaften-und-geographie/Studium/Studierende/Angewandte-Geowissenschaften-und-Georess/~lemb/Sprachkenntnisse/lidx/1/

german_requirements.notes: Some modules are only offered in German, but all compulsory courses are taught in English and the specialisation can be taken entirely in English.

course_content_summary: The Applied Geosciences MSc program at RWTH Aachen University offers five streams: Applied Mineralogy and Crystallography (AMC), Computational Geology & Geophysics (CGG), Energy and Mineral Resources (EMR), Field-based Geosciences (FBG), and Geoengineering (GIN). The Geoengineering (GIN) specialization focuses on future-oriented topics like geothermal reservoirs, earthquake prevention, landslide mitigation, permafrost, groundwater protection, and underground pollutant removal. It combines engineering geology, hydrogeology, and applied geophysics. Compulsory courses cover rock deformation in dam sites, underground construction (caverns, tunnels, geothermal boreholes), and geophysical exploration methods. PC courses teach environmental and climate data processing. Profile areas include geophysical methods, geothermal topics, modern data processing, machine learning, AI, laboratory and field courses, and groundwater modelling. Specializations in soil and groundwater remediation and water well drilling and development are also offered, with practitioner involvement. All compulsory courses are taught in English, allowing the specialization to be taken entirely in English, though some modules are only offered in German.

Distance learning program in Applied IT Security (MSc) focusing on IT security and information security. Core subjects include cryptography, discrete mathematics, network security, system security, and software engineering. Offers a broad scope of elective subjects. Emphasizes applying engineering principles to real-world problems, often from industry consultations, to strengthen development and problem-solving skills.

program_name: Applied Mathematics for Network and Data Sciences

english_requirements.notes: If you have successfully passed previous degree studies in English, you do not need proof of language proficiency.

german_requirements.notes: German language skills are not required, but they are very useful for daily life outside the university. We offer German language courses outside the programme at no extra cost.

course_content_summary: Modern mathematics, artificial intelligence, digital communication, forensics, e-commerce, medical technology, social networks, machine learning applications, Industry 4.0, data sciences, web-based services, mathematical concepts, modelling, algorithms, technologies, mathematical knowledge and methods, algorithmic view, technology-driven field, industrial relevance, innovation, computational intelligence, network analysis, data sciences, machine learning, statistical data analysis, cryptanalysis, cryptography, graph theory, simulation, visualisation, modern analysis for statistical learning theory, programming, presentation skills, project work, discrete mathematics, stochastic processes, biomathematics, Master's thesis.

program_name: Applied Methods of Social Science Research (MA)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: including at least 5 ECTS credits in quantitative methods and/or statistics

course_content_summary: The Master of Arts in Applied Methods of Social Research (AMSR) equips students with advanced, broad, and research-oriented methodological expertise for independent research in the social sciences. The program deepens understanding of research design, data collection, and both qualitative and quantitative analysis methods, building on foundational knowledge in descriptive and inferential statistics. Students expand their toolkit with advanced research designs and specialised methods, including data visualisation, statistical techniques, and qualitative discourse analysis. The curriculum covers key research fields such as Inequality and Diversity, Digital Societies, and International Politics and Conflict. The program prepares students for independent, theory-driven investigation of socially and politically relevant issues, culminating in a Master’s thesis, and is suited for those intending to pursue a PhD.

english_requirements.notes: Refer to HSRM website for accepted certificates.

course_content_summary: Students learn to transform abstract knowledge into engineering solutions in interdisciplinary teams to solve physical-technical problems. The research-oriented degree program qualifies for tasks in applied research. Course content is taught in cooperation with renowned research institutes, covering modern research-intensive fields of technology such as photonics, quantum technologies, micro- and nanotechnology, and hydrogen and energy storage technologies. The degree offers the opportunity for doctoral studies.

The Applied Research in Computer Science MSc program focuses on digitalisation, changing business models, service provision, and open innovation. It involves working with latest technologies such as machine learning, artificial intelligence, cyber-physical systems, smart data, data analytics, virtual reality, augmented reality, autonomous cars, digital mobility, digital healthcare, and the Internet of Things. The curriculum covers usability, speech recognition, natural language processing, and industry challenges. Students engage in research projects within the Institute of Information Systems (iisys), special lectures in research methodology, computer science subjects, organisation science, and business law. The program combines practical work like programming and social skills training with conceptual work such as creating architecture models, criteria-based comparisons, and literature reviews for algorithms and research approaches. Graduates can pursue PhD studies or receive support for startups.

program_name: Architecture – Design for the Built Environment (MA)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Undergraduate degree (Bachelor's or 'Diplom') in Architecture with at least 210 credit points. If Bachelor's degree comprises 180 credit points, an additional 30 credit points must be acquired by taking a preliminary course. This course entails practical experience in an architectural office in Germany as well as a design project. The practical pre-course must be started and successfully completed no later than the winter semester to ensure entry into the regular programme for the following summer semester.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, the required test may be waived. Personal interviews may be conducted to test English skills.

course_content_summary: Reflexive approach towards architecture and the city, developing architectural ideas, concepts, theories, and implementing them in architectural design within a specific urban fabric. Project-based teaching methods, field trips, research projects, academic events (summer schools, conferences).

program_name: Arqus Joint Master's Programme in European Studies
course_content_summary: Multidisciplinary understanding of Europe's role in the world, Europeanisation, globalisation, impact of global developments on Europe, changing role of the continent in the new world order. Draws on humanities and social sciences approaches. Focuses on European people, governments, companies, and civil society as global actors. Research-based analysis, future-oriented projects, scientific and political debates. Joint Master's Programme of the Arqus European University Alliance, involving University of Granada, University of Graz, Leipzig University, and Vilnius University. European Studies, joint degree programme.

required_degree_domains: Mathematics with a focus on Computer Sciences

accepted_tests.test_name: Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a higher education entrance qualification from Australia, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, Great Britain including Northern Ireland, or the United States of America do not have to submit separate proof of English language skills. Applicants who completed a secondary level degree in English in Germany or in one of the above mentioned countries are also exempted from presenting a formal English language certificate. Applicants who have acquired their higher education entrance qualification in Germany or at a German school abroad can also submit a certificate of higher education entrance qualification that shows English language skills at least at level B2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR). This proof can be provided, for example, by presenting an A-level certificate with corresponding confirmation from the school or by providing a document issued by the Ministry of Education in the respective federal state. This certificate must confirm that the required level of English (minimum of B2/CEFR) has been achieved.

german_requirements.notes: German language skills are not required for admission to this study programme.

course_content_summary: The Artificial Intelligence MSc program focuses on understanding and designing algorithms, mastering fundamental research knowledge and techniques to develop future AI methods. It combines computer science, psychology, and mathematics to research and design complex AI procedures such as Deep Learning, Support Vector Machines, Markov Random Fields, Wavelets, Bayesian Networks, optimal reasoning, knowledge representation, pattern recognition, multi-scale representation, programme verification, and constraint-based programming. The curriculum emphasizes understanding and explaining mathematical and algorithmic models, theoretical computer science, predicting system behavior, and assessing the limits and implications of AI in socially relevant problems. Professional fields include Data Science, Mathematical data analysis, Medical data analysis, development of AI algorithms and methods (e.g., machine learning), mathematical and scientific treatment of AI questions, Autonomous driving and assistance systems, Intelligent control systems (supply chains, energy, environmental sectors), and collaboration in universities and research departments.

Artificial intelligence (AI) research, mathematical AI foundations, hands-on programming, compulsory core courses, specialisation courses, thesis, machine learning, natural language processing, deep learning, computer vision, parallel computing, fairness, intelligent agents.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of having passed the examination must be submitted by the time of enrolment at the latest.

course_content_summary: Master's degree in Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or related fields. Focuses on development and design of AI methods and concepts, implementation and application of AI solutions, deep learning algorithms (especially for language and images), efficient data management concepts, AI security, and the influence of AI on society and economy. Prepares for careers in industry or science (e.g., doctorate) in AI development and application.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Business Master's program focuses on applying modern AI technologies to business management and digital innovation. Develop expertise in business processes, digital systems, strategic leadership, and AI implementation across technical, strategic, and ethical dimensions. Work on real-world projects using tools like ChatGPT, Google Vertex AI, Power BI. Master decision modelling, low-code development, agile methodologies like Design Thinking, and cultivate creative thinking and future skills for transformation and innovation initiatives.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Includes analysis, linear algebra, stochastics

english_requirements.notes: If your Bachelor's was taught exclusively in English, a confirmation of the university is sufficient.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in AI at FAU focuses on artificial intelligence, offering a broad and deep understanding intersecting with other disciplines. It covers a variety of topics, specialisations, applications, and research groups. The program emphasizes hands-on project work and practical sessions, focusing on constructive modelling and implementation of algorithms, data structures, neural networks, and their applications. The final semester is dedicated to the Master’s thesis, introducing students to research work.

The Master's program in Artificial Intelligence Aided Mobility Design prepares students to work at the intersection of design and engineering, blending creative insight with technical expertise. It focuses on utilizing the latest AI tools for innovation, speed, and precision in exploring advanced concepts for the future of mobility. The program attracts both technology-oriented designers and design-savvy engineers, equipping them with a future-proof skill set to bridge design and engineering. Graduates become proficient in cutting-edge AI techniques, developing innovative and sustainable solutions for complex challenges. Training emphasizes working seamlessly within multidisciplinary teams, communicating across various fields, and understanding both technical and aesthetic aspects of design. Close collaboration with industry partners ensures practical skills. Graduates are prepared for roles in industrial design, as studio engineers, or coordinating creative development processes at the intersection of design and engineering.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Previous higher education programme subject areas
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: each amounting to at least 5 ECTS from a previous higher education programme

german_requirements.notes: A1 certificate can be submitted until the end of the first semester.

course_content_summary: Artificial Intelligence, Data Analytics, data-based enterprise, big data, knowledge conversion, fact-based corporate decision-making, marketing, logistics, controlling, customer relationship management.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Curricula must include Analysis I + II, Linear Algebra I, and either Stochastics I or Numerics I, taught at a level corresponding to computer scientists at HHU.

english_requirements.notes: German Abitur certificate, showing English has been constantly taken as a subject and passed with the grade of 'sufficient' up to the end of qualification level 1 (grade 11 at G8-Abitur, otherwise grade); Proof that an academic degree (Bachelor's or Master's) was taught entirely in English, confirmed by a 'medium of instruction' certificate or transcript.

course_content_summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI), Data Science, digitalisation, automatisation, theoretical foundations, real-world problems, natural language processing, computer vision, analysis of biological/medical data, machine learning methods, deep learning, mathematical foundations, statistical foundations, AI-related research, state-of-the-art algorithms, research in academic environments, R&D environments, data-based IT problems in industry.

program_name: Artificial Intelligence and Data Science for Digital Business Management – Freiburg Campus

english_requirements.notes: No test required if previous high school or university education was conducted in English for at least one year (two semesters).

course_content_summary: International Master's in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science for Digital Business Management (AIM). Combines Digital Business Management, Computer Science, Data Science, and Artificial Intelligence. Programming skills are not required.

Master's degree in Artificial Intelligence and Robotics focusing on AI, machine learning, deep learning, and intelligent robotics solutions. Modules cover Deep Generative Models, Variational Autoencoders, Deep Reinforcement Learning, Simultaneous Localisation and Mapping, Robot Imitation Learning. Develops skills in planning, executing, and evaluating complex projects. Career prospects include Robotics application developer, AI developer, IT project manager, Consultant, ML architect, Data engineer, Machine learning engineer.

required_degree_domains: computer science (technical computer science, applied computer science, practical computer science, media computer science, medical computer science)
comparable computer science courses with a technical orientation

english_requirements.notes: Proof is not required if the previous degree was taught and examined in English.

german_requirements.notes: Proof of German A2 must be submitted after your first study year at the latest.

course_content_summary: Master's program in Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Industrial Applications. Focuses on deep insights into AI, addressing current topics like machine learning, natural language processing, and computer vision. Teaches solid theoretical principles and current research. Emphasizes practical, application-oriented education through designing and prototyping AI systems in projects. Covers data analysis, training, and evaluation of AI models. Utilizes modern AI labs with latest technologies and high-performance GPU computers. Elective modules explore applications in smart factories, energy sector, and medical technology to prepare for diverse career opportunities in the digital working world.

english_requirements.notes: Degree taught in English accepted

course_content_summary: Interdisciplinary, research-oriented, international Master's program in Artificial Intelligence for Molecular Sciences (AIMS). Specializations: Chemical Synthesis and Drug Design, Spectroscopy and Imaging, Data-Driven Biology. Focus on data science, artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning. Develop and apply AI models for physical simulations, analysis of high-dimensional data on molecular structure and dynamics. Prepares molecular scientists for data science.

The Master's programme in Arts and Cultural Management focuses on international cultural management, combining cultural and business studies. Key topics include social, political, and cultural transformation processes, management and finance, culture marketing, communication, branding, organisational development, audience development, international partnerships, market and business analysis, reception of culture and cultural theory, law in cultural organisations, sustainability, social responsibility, and culture policy. The program emphasizes practical skills through case studies and projects.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Astrophysics and Particle Physics relevant knowledge
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Proof of knowledge required in these areas from previous degree.

english_requirements.notes: Native speaker, English-language Bachelor's degree, or English as the first language at the German "Abitur" level accepted.

course_content_summary: The Master of Science programme in Astro and Particle Physics is an international research-oriented two-year Master's programme established by the Kepler Center, part of the Physics Department at the University of Tübingen. It involves scientists from the Institute for Astronomy and Astrophysics, the Physical Institute, and the Institute for Theoretical Physics. Research focuses on Astronomy and Astrophysics, Astroparticle Physics, and Particle Physics. The program connects science from particle physics, astrophysics, and cosmology, combining experimental and theoretical physics, astronomy, and astrophysics. Scientists use various methods to discover the origin, structure, and evolution of our universe and the properties of elementary particles under extreme conditions. A coordinated PhD programme on Particles, Fields and Messengers of the Universe is also managed by the Kepler Center.

Research-oriented Master's degree, Astrophysics, structure and evolution of the universe, cosmic phenomena, physical processes, methodological toolkit, explore and analyse phenomena, core fields: stars, stellar evolution, galaxies, cosmology, specialised topics: galaxy clusters, large-scale structures, interstellar gas, intergalactic gas, stellar winds, solar physics, cosmic magnetic fields, planetary science, astroparticle physics, gravitational waves, astronomical instrumentation, observation methods, computer simulations.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Natural Sciences, Engineering Sciences or Mathematics
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: at least 60 CP in total from these areas

accepted_tests.test_name: Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE)

english_requirements.notes: Proof of at least seven years of successfully completed English courses at a secondary German school

course_content_summary: Atmospheric Science, physics, atmosphere, climate, environment, weather, computer simulations, satellites, physical theory, climate change, severe weather warnings, renewable energies, geoscience, climate-related subjects, interdisciplinary education, international research, Meteorological Institute, Max Planck Institute for Meteorology, KlimaCampus Hamburg network.

Automation & Control, Logic Control, Nonlinear and Adaptive Controlling, Digital Signal Processing, Robust Control, Mechatronic Systems, Lab Automation and Control, Methods of Soft-Control, Modelling and Identification, Seminar, EIT department modules, RPTU department modules, individual projects, industry projects.

Automation technology, information technology (IT), hardware, software, industrial automation, computer science, Modelling and Simulation, Smart Automation and Robotics, Optimisation, Data Science, Machine Learning, AI, Industrial Communication, Industrial IT, IT Security.

Robotics, Process Automation (biochemical systems), Cognitive Systems (control systems for collaborative systems), basic control theory, sophisticated specialisations, collaborative operation, real time video processing, image processing, advanced communication between system components, AI-based control systems, mathematical proficiency, computer systems.

english_requirements.notes: Completion of undergraduate studies in the English language accepted.

german_requirements.notes: No requirement to formally prove German language skills for your admission. You may start with little or no German language proficiency.

course_content_summary: Automotive Production Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, AI, Production, New Technologies, Automotive Industry, International Communication, Cooperation, Global Competition, Digitalisation, Technology, Online Format, Hybrid Format.

accepted_tests.min_score: Listening and Reading Test 785, Speaking Test 160, Writing Test 150

english_requirements.notes: Completed degree in English Studies or American Studies accepted. Completed degree with English as the language of instruction accepted. Proof of professional qualification as state examined interpreter/translator accepted. Applicants from countries with English as official/educational or native language are not required to submit proof of English language proficiency.

german_requirements.notes: Courses to obtain the required level of German language (A2 by the end of the third semester) are included within the curriculum for non-native speakers. Students providing a certificate of A2 level (CEFR) may choose advanced language courses or subject studies.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in Automotive Software Engineering is a computer science-oriented, research-oriented program focusing on highly complex hardware/software systems for vehicles and aeroplanes. It teaches specialized skills in software engineering and system design, integrating research-related teaching from the Embedded Self-Organising Systems (ESS) field. The curriculum includes a research-relevant seminar and a research internship or project in the third semester. Students can specialize in areas like Automotive Software Platforms or Software Verification.

English-taught Master's program in Automotive Software Engineering, three semesters. Builds on Bachelor's in computer science, electrical and information technology. Focuses on software development for autonomous driving, image understanding, artificial intelligence, embedded systems, in-car communication, and Car2X communication. Emphasizes comprehensive theory and research-oriented education in state-of-the-art laboratories.

english_requirements.notes: Institutional TOEFL not accepted. Native English speakers and applicants who completed their undergraduate degrees in English (certification from university) are exempt. Applicants from India do not need to submit proof of English language proficiency.

german_requirements.notes: Basic knowledge (A1) of German required upon arrival in September. Proof of German language skills at level A2 must be provided by the end of the second semester. Beginners must take German language courses in their home countries.

course_content_summary: The Master of Engineering (MEng) in Automotive Systems (ASM) at Esslingen University of Applied Sciences focuses on developing highly-qualified engineers in automotive systems, feedback control systems, and software-intensive systems. The program, restructured in 2023 (ASM 2.0), emphasizes practical experience and industry collaboration. It offers two majors: 'Vehicle Systems' for developing and testing innovative automotive functions (steering, suspension, powertrain, ride, handling, stability, driveability, fuel economization) and 'Automotive IT' for developing and testing car communication systems, safe software, robotics, and autonomous driving. The curriculum includes networked projects in multicultural teams and interlinked courses with German Automotive Master's programs.

university_name: Coburg University of Applied Sciences and Arts

english_requirements.notes: Bachelor's programme entirely taught in the English language accepted.

course_content_summary: Practical and project-centred learning, interdisciplinary teams, agile project management, human-centred product development, sensors, actuators, vehicle networking, data processing, artificial intelligence, human-machine interfaces, Master's thesis with companies.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Physics and Mathematics must collectively total 18 CP, with individual minimums of 5 CP for Physics and 8 CP for Mathematics.

english_requirements.notes: While B2 is the minimum level required, a C1 level or higher is positively evaluated for admission. Specific accepted certificates and required minimum scores are listed elsewhere.

german_requirements.notes: It is recommended that candidates submit their German A1 language certificate at the time of admission. In case this isn’t possible, they must submit it by the end of the second semester.

course_content_summary: Master's degree in Battery Materials and Technology focusing on rapidly evolving fields of battery technology, sustainable energy storage, and electromobility. The interdisciplinary program covers the entire battery value chain, from electrode and separator materials to battery packs in e-cars. It provides scientific specifics and competencies in battery technology, offering career prospects in industry and research. Taught in English with a natural sciences focus, students can also participate in modules from the German-taught 'Batterietechnik' program with an engineering background. The program is suitable for PhD candidates and benefits from close links to the Bavarian Centre for Battery Technology (BayBatt).

required_degree_domains: Industrial Engineering with a focus on Electrical Engineering or Information Technology
Materials Science, including recycling, raw materials management or Circular Economy

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a school-leaving qualification or first degree in English issued by an institution in the United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand, Canada, or the US are exempt.

german_requirements.notes: No German language skills are required for admission and enrolment. German language courses are available as part of the curriculum.

course_content_summary: The MSc Battery Systems Engineering (BSE) program focuses on battery engineering, covering the entire battery value chain from materials science to real-world applications. It provides deep understanding of electrochemical functions, battery production, recycling, battery electronics, and diagnostics. The program is interdisciplinary and research-oriented, designed for graduates in natural and engineering sciences. It includes three semesters of lectures, exercises, practical courses, and a fourth semester for the Master's thesis. Modules are block-organized with a flexible digital teaching concept. International applicants must complete the German Engineering College (GEC) program for academic preparation.

program_name: Behaviour: From Neural Mechanisms to Evolution, MSc

english_requirements.notes: English as first language, first degree obtained at an English-speaking institution

german_requirements.notes: working knowledge of German is necessary for daily private activities, German courses are strongly recommended.

program_name: Behavioural Neurobiology / Neuroethology, Master of Science in Biology

english_requirements.notes: Bachelor's degree from an English-taught course accepted. Certificate issued by the Faculty of Biology's admission committee after approving English skills based on assessment of a Bachelor's thesis written in English, a language course other than specified above, or an interview held in English accepted.

german_requirements.notes: German language skills (e.g. B1 CEFR) are highly recommended and may be achieved during the first phase of the programme.

course_content_summary: The program covers neurobiology, behavioural physiology, sociobiology, neurogenetics, and behaviour, including interaction in social groups. Students learn principles of brain control over behaviour, chronobiology, and endogenous clocks. It emphasizes integrative approaches for complex correlations in behavioural biology, providing insight into current research. The curriculum includes theoretical and experimental skills, with two theory modules and a practical course unit per topic. Specialised research training is offered, involving students in ongoing research projects to independently plan, perform, summarise, and discuss theoretical and experimental work.

university_name: HWR Berlin (Berlin School of Economics and Law)
course_content_summary: Leadership skills, international management positions, international business environment, Finance, Strategy, Economics, Human Resources, Information Management, Marketing, Production, Organisation, basic management skills, international issues, company project, Soft skills training, career coaching, international study visits, guest lectures, company visits, Entrepreneurship, Design Thinking, Digital Business, Technology Management, Sustainable Business Leadership, Sustainable Finance, Consulting, Supply Chain Management.

university_name: HWR Berlin (Berlin School of Economics and Law)
course_content_summary: Part-time MBA program with an international perspective, focusing on leadership skills and management fundamentals. Core modules cover key management knowledge, company areas, and process/solution-oriented thinking. Elective modules allow focus on individual interests, including international management, business frameworks, strategies, management tools for multinational corporations. Specific modules include Transformation Management and Change, Digital Business and Technology Management (digital agility), Innovative Sustainability (innovation projects, partner university Porto Business School), and Entrepreneurship and Innovation (start-ups, corporates, practice-based project with Barton School of Business, Wichita, Kansas, focusing on internationalisation challenges for start-ups and SMEs). Teaching emphasizes practical, hands-on experience through corporate projects, case studies, living case studies, job-based project and group work, business simulations, and presentations from corporate leaders.

accepted_tests.test_name: Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English

english_requirements.notes: Applicants are exempt who can show that they have achieved the matriculation standard for a university from an English-speaking school and/or the completion of a university degree in a programme conducted at least partially in the English language.

german_requirements.notes: Sufficient knowledge of Hebrew and Greek to work on source texts in their original languages (proof of completed language courses in Biblical Hebrew and New Testament Greek) is required for the program content.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in Biblical Studies (MBSt) focuses on the study of methods for analysis and interpretation of original language texts from the Hebrew Bible and New Testament. It covers literary and reception history, important topics and traditions of various theological approaches, and includes an elective module for expanding historical knowledge or deepening current topics. The program prepares students for doctoral studies in Protestant Theology, particularly in Old Testament (OT) and New Testament (NT) studies, by deepening biblical knowledge through work on source texts in original languages (Hebrew, Greek) and exploring PhD research fields during Master's theses. Discussion of current research questions is integrated into all modules. Competences acquired include biblical exegesis, intercultural skills, and participation in international academic discourse. The program is offered by the Faculty of Protestant Theology in cooperation with the Centre for Religion and Society, providing interdisciplinary perspectives.

program_name: Binational Double Master's in Biomedical Sciences (IMBS)

english_requirements.notes: Proven higher education in English or a reasonably long stay in an English-speaking country are also acceptable.
