course_content_summary: In-depth and specialised knowledge in analytical chemistry, bioanalysis, quality assurance, pharmaceutical analysis, clinical analysis, and forensics. Key topics include: Analysis of biomolecules, Pharmacology and toxicology, Clinical and biochemical analysis, Methods of structural analysis, Analytical Data Science, pharmacophore models, Lab course on forensics, food and pharmaceutical analysis, Advanced analysis methods including large molecule separation, Pharmaceutical analysis and drug discovery. Prepares students for professional roles in international settings and globally operating companies.

university_name: Hochschule Fresenius - University of Applied Sciences

english_requirements.notes: Native English speakers exempt

course_content_summary: The Master’s programme in Biochemical Engineering (MEng) provides essential knowledge in chemical and biochemical engineering, mechanical process engineering, and reaction kinetics. Students engage in independent scientific projects, apply engineering practices, and adhere to GMP and regulatory standards pertinent to (bio)chemical processes. Practical expertise in analytical methods and advanced engineering methodologies relevant to industrial settings are acquired. Key topics include: Biochemical Process Engineering, Mechanical Process Engineering and Fluid Mechanics, Biomedical and Statistical Data Science, Thermal Process Engineering, Process Optimisation and Statistical Design of Experiments, Business Administration for Chemists, Upstream and Downstream Processing, Good Manufacturing Practice and Regulatory Affairs. The program prepares students for globally operating companies and entry-level positions in relevant fields.

Biopharmaceutical engineering, cell products, pharmaceutical industry, process data interpretation, biopharmaceuticals production improvement, design and operation of pharmaceutical production systems, mathematical models, computer tools, simulation, optimisation, process development, monitoring, regulatory requirements, drug production, Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), serialisation guidelines, anti-counterfeiting measures.

Biochemistry Master's program focusing on molecular foundations of biological processes, manipulation of biological pathways and biomolecules for technological applications. Tracks include Chemical Biology and Technical Biochemistry, covering biocatalysis, natural product chemistry, molecular cell biology, genome engineering, and bioanalytics. Coursework includes mandatory classroom and laboratory modules, a mandatory research internship, elective courses, and an experimental Master's thesis.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: minimum of three modules, at least 30 ECTS in total

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers exempt. English courses at a German Gymnasium (Abitur) or Bachelor's thesis in English accepted as waiver.

course_content_summary: The Master's in Biochemistry at the University of Bonn focuses on modern life sciences, exploring molecular bases of complex processes in cells, organs, and organisms using biochemical methods. It links chemistry, biology, physiology, and biophysics, with a strong emphasis on chemical biology. The program incorporates technological breakthroughs like high-resolution light microscopy, mass spectrometry, new chemical labeling methods, and robotics. It offers theoretical and practical education through lecture series, method-oriented courses, and lab rotations at LIMES and related laboratories. Key topics include chemical biology of nucleic acids, lipids, lipidomics, metabolomics, membrane protein biophysics, and the biochemical basis of neurophysiology. Students utilize state-of-the-art instrumentation and methods, supervised by internationally renowned scientists, with connections to strong research environments in immunology and metabolism.

Laboratory skills, organisational skills, practical proficiency in research, personal skills, broad degree, flexible program, international, career-oriented, English instruction, writing courses, presenting courses, international student body, worldwide peer network, lab rotations abroad, fundamentals of life and disease, nucleic acids, proteins, lipids, sugars, cell formation, organism formation, disease mechanisms, drug development, combines biology and chemistry, topics include immunology, cell biology, molecular biology, structural biology, biophysics, virology, microbiology, pathobiochemistry, toxicology, pharmacology, bioinformatics, developmental biology, neuroscience, molecular medicine.

International Master's program in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology focusing on research topics from molecules to cells, animal, and plant life. Core expertise in biomedicine, molecular plant sciences, evolutionary systems biology, and macromolecular systems. Students engage in research using genomics, genetics, systematic bio- and protein analysis, and high-end microscopy. Program is embedded in collaborative interdisciplinary research networks, including Max Planck and Fraunhofer Institutes.

english_requirements.notes: Bachelor's degree obtained not longer than two years ago in a programme taught in English is accepted.

german_requirements.notes: Required for applicants intending to study the programme completely in German. Bachelor's degree obtained not longer than two years ago in a programme taught in German is accepted.

course_content_summary: Master's programme in Biochemistry offered by departments of biology and chemistry, Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf (HHU) and Research Centre Jülich. Focus on current research topics. Comprises two semesters, 30 credit points each. First semester: lectures, practical training. Optional three-month research internship. Second semester: Master's thesis, six-month research project, written and oral presentation.

The Master's program in Bioeconomy focuses on sectors utilizing biological resources and biotechnological processes for bio-based goods and services. Students examine the entire value chain of bio-based products, including raw material production in agricultural ecosystems, properties and supply logistics of biological resources, biotechnological and industrial conversion processes, and marketing and consumption. The program addresses environmental, social, and economic dimensions of the bioeconomy from micro- and macro-level perspectives, considering innovation, institutions, and policies. It is an interdisciplinary program offered by the faculties of Agricultural Sciences, Natural Sciences, and Business, Economics and Social Sciences, enabling systematic analysis of bio-based value chains. Graduates gain expertise to understand bio-based value chains from the perspectives of companies focusing on new resources/processes/products, established producers introducing renewable resources/biotechnological processes, actors gauging need/acceptance, and organizations supporting development through research/advisory services.
